clinical_summary: Autosomal recessive neurovisceral disorder with progressive neurologic features and variable onset.

phenotypes.evidence_span: Affected individuals may present with progressive neurologic deterioration, ataxia, vertical supranuclear gaze palsy, dysphagia, seizures, and hepatosplenomegaly.
Affected individuals may present with progressive neurologic deterioration, ataxia, vertical supranuclear gaze palsy, dysphagia, seizures, and hepatosplenomegaly.
Affected individuals may present with progressive neurologic deterioration, ataxia, vertical supranuclear gaze palsy, dysphagia, seizures, and hepatosplenomegaly.

age_of_onset.evidence_span: Onset is variable and often occurs in childhood or adolescence.

inheritance.evidence_span: Niemann-Pick disease type C1 is an autosomal recessive neurovisceral disorder.

progression.evidence_span: Affected individuals may present with progressive neurologic deterioration
